summary: First iteration. Tested backend (12 pytest cases, 100% pass) and frontend (planner UI + save/load/delete flow). Backend API contracts all verified: categories, KML extraction & error handling, optimize-route greedy+2opt, itineraries CRUD with proper date serialization and no ObjectId leak. Frontend UI loads, all data-testids present, sliders/switch interactive, invalid-URL toast works, save/load/delete dialogs wired to backend. Live Google My Maps import could not be verified end-to-end because Google returns 404 for both provided sample map URLs (not a bug in our backend — confirmed via direct curl to www.google.com/maps/d/kml). Backend correctly returns a clean 400 with Spanish detail message in this case.

issues: Grammar: 'X paradas omitidas' is shown even when X=1; should be singular 'parada omitida'. Minor i18n nit.

action_items: (Optional) Pluralize the 'paradas omitidas' banner in MapPanel based on count.

(Optional) The provided sample My Maps URLs (mid=1Pl9VRpkqJUmM52Vdr4_4mUBP-cb-XJDB and mid=12VxCAY2pq3ZkjxepIeijuIWyMOk) return 404 from google.com/maps/d/kml. Either share a confirmed-public map or instruct users to verify public sharing. Backend behavior is correct.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is currently ~460 lines and fine; if more features are added consider splitting models/routes/optimizer into modules.
extract_map_id fallback `re.fullmatch(r'[A-Za-z0-9_\-]{20,}', url.strip())` will accept many random tokens as a 'mid'; acceptable since downstream KML fetch will fail.
list_itineraries correctly excludes _id with projection and converts created_at ISO string back to datetime.
OptimizeResponse 2-opt loop has O(n^3) but with iter_guard 30; fine for typical My Maps (~50 points) but document the upper bound.
seed_data_creation: Test itineraries are created with name prefix 'TEST_' or 'TEST_UI_' and deleted in fixture teardown / end-of-test cleanup. Zero leftover after run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend at https://tour-scheduler-6.preview.emergentagent.com. Pytest file at /app/backend/tests/test_planner_api.py runs in ~2s. Both provided sample My Maps URLs return 404 from Google directly, so live KML success path can't be E2E-tested in sandbox; use a freshly shared public My Maps URL or rely on the 400 error path + synthetic itinerary seeding (via POST /api/itineraries with name 'TEST_UI_*' for cleanup).
rca of the issue: No bug. The Google My Maps URLs supplied in the request both return HTTP 404 from https://www.google.com/maps/d/kml?mid=... (verified with direct curl from the container). Backend logs no error — it correctly raises HTTPException(400, 'No se pudo descargar el KML...'). Resolution: provide a known-public My Maps share link, or accept the documented limitation.
